title: rubus subtomentosiformis
intro: Rubus subtomentosiformis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan raspberry shrub, is a species of thorny shrub belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus subtomentosiformis include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, cancer, and cognitive decline, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function. Rubus subtomentosiformis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
